name: Eggplant Gratin
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 pounds Ripe tomatoes (peeled, seeded and chopped)
10 large Basil leaves, torn into small pieces
3 ounces Gruyere cheese (shredded or thinly sliced)
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice eggplants diagonally about 1/2-inch thick. Discard pieces that are all skin on one side. If using round eggplants, halve them lengthwise, then cut into 1/2 or 1/4 rounds, about 1/2-inch thick.
2. Generously cover the bottom of a wide skillet with oil. When hot, add eggplant in a single layer and fry until golden on both sides and tender. Drain on paper towels. Add more oil if needed, and fry the rest of the eggplant.
3. FOR SAUCE: Warm olive oil in the skillet and add onion, garlic, and herbs. Stir to coat onion with oil. Cook gently until onions soften, 12 to 15 minutes.
4. Add chopped tomatoes, salt lightly, and raise heat. Cook, stirring occasionally, until juice has evaporated and sauce is thick, about 15 minutes. Season to taste with salt and pepper. If tomatoes are on the acid side, add sugar to taste to correct balance.
5. FOR CUSTARD: Cover saffron threads with a few tablespoons hot water and let stand for a few minutes. In a bowl, beat eggs and stir in ricotta cheese, milk, and Parmesan cheese. Season with salt and pepper, then stir in saffron with liquid.
6. FOR GRATIN: Spread a little tomato sauce over the bottom of a baking dish. Arrange a layer of eggplant slices, slightly overlapping, on top. Season with salt and pepper, scatter half the basil, add Gruyere. Make another basil-eggplant layer.
7. Cover with the rest of the tomato sauce and pour custard over the top. Preheat oven to 350°F and bake for 40 minutes or until custard sets and is a delicate golden brown. Remove from oven and allow to rest a few minutes before serving.
